no_changes_rationale:
Epic ddx-e34994e2 (Story 10: automatic escalation + always-review) has no implementation work of its own. Its acceptance criterion is literally "All child beads closed."

Current child status (parent=ddx-e34994e2):
  closed: ddx-8f1ac866 (fizeau CONTRACT-003 extension)
  open:   ddx-51ac997b (escalation ladder.Next + tests)
  open:   ddx-cfedee8e (wire ladder into executor closure)
  open:   ddx-2436fa73 (BuildReviewExecuteRequest + remove implHarness default)
  open:   ddx-a8718bec (charge reviewer cost to cap)
  open:   ddx-86996834 (--no-review break-glass + review:skip rationale)
  open:   ddx-e0be88f6 (Step 0 heightened hint + depth-2 cap)
  open:   ddx-0ca4ffda (routinglint guard)
  open:   ddx-5f1eac4f (FEAT-006/010/014 + ADR-021)

What is done: 1/9 child beads closed (the upstream Fizeau contract bead, which unblocks several others).

What is blocking: 8 child beads remain open. Per the planning round's locked decisions, those children carry the testable ACs; the epic is a tracker, not a unit of implementation. Bundling all of them into one attempt would violate the size-check guidance in the execute-bead instructions (multiple feature-sized pieces, >500 LOC across unrelated packages, AC mixes design/impl/tests/docs).

What a follow-up attempt would need: nothing at the epic level. The execute-loop should drain the children individually (most are tier:cheap or tier:standard; ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-2436fa73 unblocked by the closed B1, so they are ready). Once all children close, this epic can be closed administratively (or by re-running execute-bead, which will then find AC satisfied).

No code changes committed in this attempt.
